response.justification: Strong software engineering fundamentals, IDE/tooling interest (LabWindows/CVI development, current MCP framework work), and systems programming background are valuable. However, the resume lacks demonstrated expertise in Java, Kotlin, and Spring—the core technical requirements. Recent work (2024-present) has shifted toward TypeScript/Node.js, with no evidence of enterprise framework (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate) experience. While architectural principles transfer, the specific JVM expertise gap is significant for a mid-senior position.
strengths: Proven IDE/tooling experience (LabWindows/CVI development) plus current MCP infrastructure work demonstrates genuine interest in developer tooling and language internals
Deep systems programming and performance optimization background (C/C++/D, low-latency systems at scale) provides relevant foundation for JVM internals and complex systems work
Consistent emphasis on software craftsmanship, TDD, testing, and clean architecture across multiple roles aligns with quality and maintainability standards
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin expertise; Java appears only as a skill with minimal project evidence, and Kotlin is entirely absent from resume
No Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate) despite role requiring solid expertise in these areas
Current trajectory away from JVM: recent work focused on TypeScript/Node.js and AI infrastructure, suggesting potential disengagement from Java/Kotlin development
